meta.title: BoatExperts — Free Marine Diagnostics Help From a Certified Tech
meta.description: Boat acting up and you're not sure why? Talk to a certified marine technician for free before you spend money guessing at parts or paying for a shop visit.

advisors.name: Frank Deluca
advisors.bio: 18 years diagnosing inboard and outboard problems across gasoline and diesel engines. Frank has a reputation for nailing the root cause on the first call — not after three parts swaps.

advisors.name: Carla Reyes
advisors.bio: ABYC-certified electrical tech with 10 years troubleshooting bilge pumps, ignition systems, charging circuits, and onboard electronics. Nothing random about boat electrics once you know where to look.

advisors.name: Ben Okafor
advisors.bio: Volvo Penta and Mercury-certified tech with 12 years in the field. If it overheats, misfires, or won't start, Ben knows the likely causes before you finish describing the symptoms.

hero.headline: Boat acting up? Find out why before you spend a dollar.
hero.subheadline: Overheating, misfiring, electrical gremlins, mysterious alarms — guessing at parts gets expensive fast. Describe the problem to a certified marine tech and get a real diagnosis, for free.
hero.form_note: Free. No commitment. We'll match you with a tech within 24 hours.

benefits.headline: Parts-swapping without a diagnosis is expensive
benefits.subheadline: The average boat owner spends $400 replacing the wrong parts before finding the real problem. A free 30-minute call usually finds it.

items.title: Stop guessing at parts
items.body: Fuel pump, thermostat, impeller, injectors — the symptoms often point to multiple possibilities. Your tech will narrow it down based on how, when, and where the problem shows up.

items.title: Know before you call a shop
items.body: Going into a marine shop without a diagnosis means paying shop rates to figure out what you could have known for free. Walk in knowing exactly what's wrong and what it should cost to fix.

items.title: Fix it yourself if you can
items.body: A lot of common boat problems are DIY-friendly once you know what you're dealing with. Your tech will tell you honestly whether it's a wrench job or something that needs professional hands.

how_it_works.headline: How it works
how_it_works.subheadline: Describe the symptom. We'll find the cause.

steps.title: Tell us about the problem
steps.body: What's happening, when it happens, how long it's been going on, and anything you've already tried. The more detail, the faster your tech can zero in.

steps.title: Walk through it with a diagnostic tech
steps.body: A certified marine technician who knows your engine type will ask the right questions on a live video call and work through the most likely causes with you.

steps.title: Get a clear answer and a fix plan
steps.body: Before the call ends you'll know what's most likely wrong, what parts you need (if any), and whether it's a DIY job or a shop job — with a realistic cost estimate.

testimonials.headline: What boaters said after their session

items.quote: Engine kept overheating at WOT. I'd already replaced the impeller. My tech asked two questions and told me it was a blocked telltale. Ten-minute fix. Saved me a $300 shop visit.
items.name: Mike Svensson

items.quote: Random bilge alarm going off mid-trip. No water in the bilge. My tech walked me through it in 20 minutes — float switch had corroded. Replaced it for $12.
items.name: Rachel Nguyen

items.quote: Had a rough idle at low RPM all season. Took it to a shop twice, they couldn't replicate it. Described it to my BoatExperts tech and he diagnosed a fuel pressure issue in 30 minutes.
items.name: Gary Thornton

form.headline: Describe your boat problem — we'll find the cause
form.subheadline: Tell us the symptoms and your engine type. Your tech will reach out within 24 hours.
